question.label: Stretch now
question.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?
question.description: Distinguishes comfort-zone execution from genuine growth moments.

answer: Sometimes I avoid high-ambiguity forums because I do not want to sound underprepared.

assessment.note: Avoiding high-ambiguity forums to avoid sounding underprepared signals a strong growth constraint and mild pullback in engagement.

new_queue.label: Ambiguity forums

new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.name: What kind of design work would make the next step feel real for you?

new_queue.name: What kind of air cover would let you take a more visible position?

new_queue.name: What would you need to stop doing to make room for stronger design leadership?

history.answer_excerpt: Sometimes I avoid high-ambiguity forums because I do not want to sound underprepared.
I am still motivated, but the promotion outcome made me more careful about where I spend political capital.

I can lead major initiatives, but I do not always translate that into visible strategic influence.
I get positive feedback on craft and collaboration, but less clarity on what separates me at the next level.
